channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Explores Coal for Stable Energy Supply and Economic Growth
item.description: Coal remains one of Tanzania's most valuable yet underused natural resources, mainly found in the southern and southwestern regions, including Njombe, Ruvuma, and Mbeya. Among the notable sites is the...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rain Across Several Regions Amid Persisting Heat
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has forecast increased rainfall across several parts of the country from April 14 to 20, even as high temperatures persist in arid and coastal regions. Western Keny...

item.title: Analysis of Trump’s Proposed Naval Blockade in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: This article presents economist Jeffrey Sachs' analysis of former U.S. President Donald Trump's proposed naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil transportation. Sa...

item.title: Federal High Court Grants ₦200 Million Bail to Former Kaduna Governor El-Rufai Amid ICPC Trial
item.description: Former Kaduna State governor, Nasir El-Rufai, has been granted bail by the Federal High Court sitting in Kaduna in relation to an ongoing trial involving allegations of fraud and money laundering brou...

item.title: Truck brake failure causes rollover accident killing 33 hogs in Sarangani
item.description: At least 33 hybrid hogs died after a truck transporting around 150 pigs overturned along a highway in Barangay Nagpan, Malungon, Sarangani on April 14, 2026. According to police and local officials, t...

item.title: US signals possible resumption of Iran talks with Pakistan as facilitator
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has indicated that negotiations between Washington and Tehran may resume within the next two days, suggesting that the United States is “more inclined” to continue dip...

item.title: Bournemouth Confirm Andoni Iraola Will Depart After 2025/26 Season
item.description: Bournemouth don announce say their head coach, Andoni Iraola, go leave the club after the 2025/26 season don finish. The Spaniard wey dey 43 years old join the Cherries from Rayo Vallecano back in 202...

item.title: COA upholds ruling against ₱73M confidential fund disbursement by Vice President’s office
item.description: The Commission on Audit (COA) has upheld its decision disallowing the Office of the Vice President’s (OVP) release of ₱73 million in confidential funds from December 21 to 31, 2022. According to COA I...

item.title: Court proceedings begin for Niagara Falls councillor Mike Strange in domestic assault case
item.description: The domestic assault trial of Niagara Falls city councillor Mike Strange began in St. Catharines, Ontario, drawing attention due to his public role and past as a Canadian Olympic boxer. Strange, 55, w...

item.title: Community protests as Dobsonville church leader appears in court over alleged rape of 12-year-old girl
item.description: On Tuesday, tensions rose outside the Roodepoort Magistrate’s Court as protesters from various political and community groups gathered for the court appearance of a Dobsonville church elder accused of...

item.title: Tanzania Expands Motorcycle Safety Programs to Reduce Road Injuries
item.description: The Tanzanian government has intensified efforts to improve road safety among motorcycle riders, aiming to reduce traffic-related injuries and fatalities. Dr Grace Magembe, Government Chief Medical Of...

item.title: Hollywood Elite Voice Concerns Over Disney-Paramount Merger and Free Speech
item.description: A significant number of Hollywood actors and creatives—over 1,400—have publicly expressed their opposition to the proposed merger between Disney and Paramount. The open letter, shared widely online, i...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Criticises BJP and Raises Concerns Over SIR Election Process in West Bengal
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has again criticised the BJP and the Election Commission over the SIR (Systematic Identification and Registration) process during a public rally in Pingla, P...

item.title: IMF lowers 2026 global growth outlook as Middle East war drives up energy prices
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has reduced its 2026 global growth forecast to 3.1 per cent, warning that the world economy could be thrown off course by the war in the Middle East. The revision comes...

item.title: North Korea conducts missile tests from new destroyer under Kim Jong Un’s supervision
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un personally observed a series of missile tests launched from the country’s newest warship, a 5,000-ton-class destroyer named Choe Hyon, according to state media reports....

item.title: Energy providers may reward households for using appliances during periods of surplus renewable power
item.description: The UK's energy system is introducing measures that could allow households and businesses to benefit from cheaper or even free electricity when there is an excess of supply. Traditionally, energy prov...

item.title: Zardari praises Pakistan's diplomatic role in US-Iran peace talks
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif at Aiwan-e-Sadr and praised Pakistan’s diplomatic efforts in hosting and facilitating dialogue between the United States and Iran. He descr...

item.title: Simba SC restore striker Jonathan Sowah under disciplinary supervision with youth team training
item.description: Simba Sports Club have decided to reinstate their Ghanaian forward Jonathan Sowah under specific disciplinary conditions. The club directed him to train with the under-20 youth squad instead of joinin...

item.title: Government strengthens infection control systems in Tanzanian health facilities
item.description: The Tanzanian government, working with the Ministry of Health, the Prime Minister’s Office – Regional Administration and Local Government (OWM-TAMISEMI), and the CIHEB-Tanzania Global Health Security ...

item.title: IMF Revises 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 3.1% Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has revised down its global growth forecast for 2026 to 3.1 per cent, citing disruptions caused by the ongoing Middle East conflict. The downgrade comes after US-...
